---
title: "Regression and Classification on FIFA WC 2022 Dataset"
author: "Argha Ghosh"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    code_folding: show
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

# ============================================================
# GLOBAL SETTINGS
# ============================================================

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
set.seed(42)

# ============================================================
# PACKAGE MANAGEMENT
# ============================================================

packages <- c("tidyverse", "ggplot2", "corrplot",
              "skimr", "DataExplorer", "caret",
              "e1071", "rpart", "rpart.plot")

not_installed <- packages[!(packages %in% rownames(installed.packages()))]
if (length(not_installed) > 0) {
  install.packages(not_installed, repos = "https://cran.rstudio.com/")
}

# ============================================================
# LOAD LIBRARIES
# ============================================================

library(tidyverse)    # Data manipulation and transformation
library(ggplot2)      # Data visualization
library(corrplot)     # Correlation matrix visualization
library(skimr)        # Extended summary statistics
library(DataExplorer) # Automated EDA and missing value plots
library(caret)        # Machine learning and model training
library(e1071)        # Support vector machines and statistics
library(rpart)        # Decision tree modeling
library(rpart.plot)   # Decision tree visualization

```

---

## 📌 1. Load Data

```{r load-data}

# ============================================================
# DATA LOADING
# Dataset  : FIFA World Cup 2022 Players Statistics
# Source   : Google Drive (shared via public link)
# Method   : download.file() to temp file — fully reproducible
# ============================================================

# Step 1: Define file ID and construct Google Drive download URL
file_id   <- "1ZQeE33xnHGuInF1vBkVbm0tZ1on_cAIA"
url       <- paste0("https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=", file_id)

# Step 2: Download to a temporary file then read — more reliable than direct URL
temp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")
download.file(url, destfile = temp_file, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)
data      <- read.csv(temp_file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Step 3: Clean column names — remove leading/trailing spaces
colnames(data) <- trimws(colnames(data))

# Step 4: Convert columns that load as character to numeric
data$Appearances                 <- as.numeric(trimws(data$Appearances))
data$FIFA.Ranking                <- as.numeric(trimws(data$FIFA.Ranking))
data$National.Team.Jersey.Number <- as.numeric(trimws(data$National.Team.Jersey.Number))

# Step 5: Convert string-numeric columns — replace "-" and "N.A" with NA
clean_num <- function(x) {
  suppressWarnings(as.numeric(ifelse(trimws(x) %in% c("-", "N.A", "N.A.", ""), NA, trimws(x))))
}

data$Goals.Scored           <- clean_num(data$Goals.Scored)
data$Assists.Provided       <- clean_num(data$Assists.Provided)
data$Dribbles.per.90        <- clean_num(data$Dribbles.per.90)
data$Interceptions.per.90   <- clean_num(data$Interceptions.per.90)
data$Tackles.per.90         <- clean_num(data$Tackles.per.90)
data$Total.Duels.Won.per.90 <- clean_num(data$Total.Duels.Won.per.90)

# Step 6: Preview the dataset
cat("Dataset loaded successfully!\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(data), "| Columns:", ncol(data), "\n\n")
head(data)

```

## 📌 2. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

### Summary

```{r eda-summary}

# ============================================================
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
# Provides overview of distributions and ranges
# ============================================================

summary(data)

```


### Distribution

```{r eda-distribution}

# ============================================================
# DISTRIBUTION OF APPEARANCES
# Shows how many matches players have played
# ============================================================

ggplot(data, aes(x = Appearances)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Player Appearances",
    x     = "Appearances",
    y     = "Count"
  )

```


### Pair Plot

```{r eda-pairplot}

# ============================================================
# PAIR PLOT OF NUMERICAL FEATURES
# Shows relationships between key numeric columns
# ============================================================

pair_cols <- data %>%
  select(FIFA.Ranking, Appearances,
         Goals.Scored, Assists.Provided, Dribbles.per.90) %>%
  na.omit()

pairs(pair_cols, main = "Pair Plot of Key Numerical Features")

```


### Correlation

```{r eda-correlation}

# ============================================================
# CORRELATION MATRIX
# Detects linear relationships between numerical features
# ============================================================

num_data   <- data %>% select_if(is.numeric)
cor_matrix <- cor(num_data, use = "complete.obs")
print(round(cor_matrix, 3))

```


## 📌 3. Data Preprocessing

```{r preprocessing}

# ============================================================
# DATA PREPROCESSING
# Step 1: Check missing values
# Step 2: Fill missing values
# Step 3: Scale numeric features
# ============================================================

# --- Step 1: Check Missing Values ---
cat("=== Missing Values Before Handling ===\n")
print(colSums(is.na(data)))

# --- Step 2: Fill Missing Values ---

# Helper function to calculate mode for categorical columns
get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x[!is.na(x)])
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

# Fill numerical columns with median (robust to outliers)
num_cols_names <- names(data)[sapply(data, is.numeric)]
for (col in num_cols_names) {
  if (any(is.na(data[[col]]))) {
    data[[col]][is.na(data[[col]])] <- median(data[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  }
}

# Fill categorical columns with mode (most frequent value)
cat_cols_names <- names(data)[sapply(data, function(x) is.character(x) | is.factor(x))]
for (col in cat_cols_names) {
  if (any(is.na(data[[col]]))) {
    data[[col]][is.na(data[[col]])] <- get_mode(data[[col]])
  }
}

cat("\n=== Missing Values After Handling ===\n")
cat("Total missing values remaining:", sum(is.na(data)), "\n")

# --- Step 3: Scale Numerical Features ---
num_cols_data   <- data %>% select_if(is.numeric)
scaled_features <- scale(num_cols_data)

# Replace original numeric columns with scaled values
data_scaled <- data
data_scaled[, names(num_cols_data)] <- scaled_features

# Check structure after scaling
str(data_scaled)

```


## 📌 4. Train-Test Split

```{r train-test-split}

# ============================================================
# TRAIN-TEST SPLIT FOR REGRESSION
# 80% training, 20% testing
# Target: Goals Scored (numerical prediction)
# ============================================================

trainIndex <- createDataPartition(data_scaled$Goals.Scored,
                                  p = 0.8, list = FALSE)

train_data <- data_scaled[trainIndex, ]
test_data  <- data_scaled[-trainIndex, ]

cat("Training set size :", nrow(train_data), "\n")
cat("Testing set size  :", nrow(test_data),  "\n")

```

---

## 📈 5. Linear Regression

```{r linear-regression}

# ============================================================
# LINEAR REGRESSION
# Target   : Goals Scored
# Features : FIFA Ranking, Appearances, Assists Provided,
#            Dribbles per 90, Tackles per 90
# ============================================================

lm_model <- lm(Goals.Scored ~ FIFA.Ranking + Appearances +
                 Assists.Provided + Dribbles.per.90 + Tackles.per.90,
               data = train_data)

summary(lm_model)

```


```{r regression-evaluation}

# ============================================================
# REGRESSION EVALUATION METRICS
# RMSE — Root Mean Square Error (lower = better)
# MAE  — Mean Absolute Error   (lower = better)
# R²   — Proportion of variance explained (higher = better)
# ============================================================

pred_lm <- predict(lm_model, newdata = test_data)
rmse    <- sqrt(mean((test_data$Goals.Scored - pred_lm)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
mae     <- mean(abs(test_data$Goals.Scored   - pred_lm),  na.rm = TRUE)
r2      <- cor(test_data$Goals.Scored, pred_lm, use = "complete.obs")^2

cat("=== Regression Evaluation Metrics ===\n")
cat("RMSE :", round(rmse, 4), "\n")
cat("MAE  :", round(mae,  4), "\n")
cat("R²   :", round(r2,   4), "\n")

```


```{r regression-plot}

# ============================================================
# ACTUAL vs PREDICTED PLOT
# Points close to the red line = accurate predictions
# ============================================================

plot(test_data$Goals.Scored, pred_lm,
     xlab = "Actual Goals Scored",
     ylab = "Predicted Goals Scored",
     main = "Actual vs Predicted — Linear Regression",
     col  = "steelblue",
     pch  = 16)
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)

```

---

## 🌳 6. Decision Tree Classification

```{r decision-tree}

# ============================================================
# DECISION TREE CLASSIFICATION
# Target   : Position (GK, DF, MF, FW)
# Features : Key numerical performance features
# Note     : Using original unscaled data — trees don't need scaling
# ============================================================

# Prepare classification dataset with Position as factor
data_cls          <- data
data_cls$Position <- as.factor(data_cls$Position)

# Classification train-test split (stratified by Position)
trainIndex2 <- createDataPartition(data_cls$Position,
                                   p = 0.8, list = FALSE)

train_cls <- data_cls[trainIndex2, ]
test_cls  <- data_cls[-trainIndex2, ]

cat("Training set size :", nrow(train_cls), "\n")
cat("Testing set size  :", nrow(test_cls),  "\n\n")

# Build decision tree model
dt_model <- rpart(Position ~ FIFA.Ranking + Appearances +
                    Goals.Scored + Assists.Provided +
                    Dribbles.per.90 + Tackles.per.90,
                  data   = train_cls,
                  method = "class")

# Visualize the decision tree
rpart.plot(dt_model, main = "Decision Tree — Player Position Classification")

```


### Feature Importance

```{r feature-importance}

# ============================================================
# FEATURE IMPORTANCE
# Shows which features the tree relies on most
# Higher value = more important for classification
# ============================================================

cat("=== Feature Importance ===\n")
print(dt_model$variable.importance)

```

---

```{r classification-evaluation}

# ============================================================
# CLASSIFICATION EVALUATION
# Confusion Matrix — correct vs incorrect predictions
# Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score per class
# ============================================================

pred_dt <- predict(dt_model, test_cls, type = "class")
confusionMatrix(pred_dt, test_cls$Position)

```
